description: Fix the Contentful field name used to extract spot tip type references in the spot mapper. The code currently uses :spot_tips (expecting Contentful field ID 'spotTips'), but based on the established naming pattern in this project (where reference fields use the content type name - e.g., paddleCraftType, spotType, spot, waterway), the actual Contentful field ID is likely 'spotTipType' which snakifies to :spot_tip_type. This was confirmed by a prior bug fix (commit 72dc317) where the obstacle mapper's spots field was changed from :spots to :spot to match the actual Contentful field name. The fix changes :spot_tips to :spot_tip_type in the mapper and updates the corresponding test to use the correct field name.
steps: 1. In _plugins/contentful_mappers.rb, line 272, change resolve_field(fields, :spot_tips, locale) to resolve_field(fields, :spot_tip_type, locale). This is the spot mapper's field lookup for spotTipType references.

2. In spec/plugins/spot_tip_type_mapper_spec.rb, update the Property 2 test ('Spot Mapper Includes spotTipType_slugs'). The test builds a fake fields hash with key :spot_tips - change this to :spot_tip_type to match the updated mapper code.

3. In docs/content-model.md, update the spot content type table. Change the field name from 'spot_tips' to 'spot_tip_type' for the References -> spotTipType[] field. This documents the actual Contentful field ID.
4. Run bundle exec rspec to verify all Ruby tests pass with the updated field name.
5. Run npm test to verify no JS tests are broken.
acceptance_criteria: The field lookup in map_spot uses :spot_tip_type instead of :spot_tips
The Property 2 test in spec/plugins/spot_tip_type_mapper_spec.rb passes with the updated field key
All existing RSpec tests pass (bundle exec rspec)
All existing Jest tests pass (npm test)
docs/content-model.md spot table shows spot_tip_type as the field name
verification: Run `bundle exec rspec` and confirm all tests pass
Run `npm test` and confirm all tests pass
